name: clearing_records
title: Clearing and settlement records (illustrative)
description: ILLUSTRATIVE / SYNTHETIC worked example. Tokugawa-period money-changer and remittance settlements, structured to demonstrate the project's coding conventions: transparent unit normalisation across the sanka (gold/silver/copper) system, disaggregated missingness, explicit uncertainty, a source-language layer, and per-observation coder attribution. Not archival data.

fields.name: record_id
fields.title: Record ID
fields.description: Stable unique identifier for the settlement record. Never reused.

fields.name: date_wareki
fields.title: Date (Japanese era, original)
fields.description: Date exactly as written in the source, in the Japanese era (wareki) calendar. Preserves the original, including markers of loss or inference (e.g. 欠 'missing', 推定 'inferred').

fields.name: date_iso
fields.title: Date (ISO 8601, normalised)
fields.description: Gregorian/ISO normalisation of date_wareki. Reduced precision (year, or year-month) is expressed by a truncated ISO string when the source does not license a full date.

fields.name: date_confidence
fields.title: Date confidence
fields.description: Confidence in the normalised date.

fields.name: instrument_wareki
fields.title: Instrument (original term)
fields.description: The instrument as named in the source, in the original script (e.g. 手形, 為替, 振替, 両替).

fields.name: instrument_romaji
fields.title: Instrument (romanisation)
fields.description: Hepburn romanisation of instrument_wareki.

fields.name: instrument_type
fields.title: Instrument type (coded)
fields.description: Controlled code mapping the source term to a project category. Vocabulary: vocabularies/instrument_type.csv. Where a term's referent shifts across periods, the shift is documented in the logbook rather than silently recoded.

fields.name: amount_original
fields.title: Amount (as written)
fields.description: The figure as recorded in the source, in its original unit (amount_unit). Missing values use the disaggregated codes (see missingness and missingValues); an explicit source zero is the number 0, not a missing code.

fields.name: amount_unit
fields.title: Original unit
fields.description: Monetary unit of amount_original within the sanka three-currency system.

fields.name: amount_monme_silver
fields.title: Amount (normalised, silver monme)
fields.description: amount_original normalised to a common unit — silver monme — to make cross-currency records comparable. The conversion applied is named in normalization_method; the choice of common unit and its assumptions are the project's, and are auditable per row.

fields.name: normalization_method
fields.title: Normalisation method
fields.description: The exact conversion applied to reach amount_monme_silver: 'identity_silver' (already silver), a named gold/copper conversion with its rate and source (e.g. 'ryo_to_monme@60_official1700'), or '.NA' where no amount exists to convert.

fields.name: missingness
fields.title: Missingness (of amount)
fields.description: Epistemic status of the amount. Four kinds of absence are never conflated: observed, not_recorded, illegible, not_applicable — plus explicit_zero for a source-recorded nil, which is a value, not an absence.

fields.name: payer
fields.title: Payer / remitter
fields.description: Party debited, as named in the source (romanised). Missing coded per convention.

fields.name: payee
fields.title: Payee / beneficiary
fields.description: Party credited, as named in the source (romanised). Missing coded per convention.

fields.name: place_romaji
fields.title: Place
fields.description: Place of settlement (romanised).

fields.name: source_ref
fields.title: Source reference
fields.description: Archive/series call number and folio, keyed to logbook 5 (quality and nature of sources).

fields.name: source_lang
fields.title: Source language
fields.description: ISO 639-1 language of the source text.

fields.name: confidence
fields.title: Record confidence
fields.description: Overall confidence in the coded record. Uncertainty is made an explicit column, not laundered into prose.

fields.name: coder
fields.title: Coder
fields.description: Initials of the team member who entered the record. The backstop for attribution is git blame; contestable interpretive calls additionally carry an authority note in the logbook.

fields.name: notes
fields.title: Notes
fields.description: Free-text coder note: rationale, caveats, cross-references.

fields.name: variant_of
fields.title: Variant of (record link)
fields.description: For a row that is an alternate coding of another record — e.g. a second normalisation retained to show rate sensitivity — the record_id it varies. Primary records carry .NA. The value must be a well-formed, existing record_id; referential integrity is enforced by the self-referencing foreign key below. Keeps the one-transaction/two-codings relation machine-readable rather than only in the notes prose.
